user_message: Give me the manager view on this Nod Publishers exec meeting notes. Renewal conversation is tomorrow.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: QBR preparation Discussion - Nod Publishers
Date: 2024-03-13
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Samantha Singh (Nod Publishers), Marcus Williams (Microsoft), Michelle (Nod Publishers)

[00:02:00] Samantha Singh (Nod Publishers): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on QBR preparation from our last session.

[00:05:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is timeline constraints.

[00:10:00] Samantha Singh (Nod Publishers): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about resource allocation. Can we address those?

[00:13:00] Michelle (Nod Publishers): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about stakeholder alignment. Can we address those?

[00:17:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): The timeline for vendor dependencies is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:19:00] Michelle (Nod Publishers): The timeline for data residency is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:22:00] Michelle (Nod Publishers): The timeline for budget approval is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF47-E trap (Invents commitments not made); produces complete Decision log with speaker attribution, action distribution by role, stakeholder map delta, MSX updat
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF47-E failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF47-E failure mode (Invents commitments not made)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct QBR preparation decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF47-E -- Invents commitments not made
